_comment: User base / profile store. 'users' maps a name -> role; 'roles' defines each role's department + the CLEARANCES it holds (security labels the role may see). Whitelist-only: NO deny list, NO wildcard — access exists only where a label is explicitly granted. A memory is visible iff ALL its labels are within the user's clearances (see bouncer.py). Note exec simply does NOT hold 'legal' — exclusion by omission, not by deny. The ben-* users are the MANUAL-TEST set (one per clearance level, for src/live_test.py): ask the same question as each ben-* and watch the MemoryLane change. Only ben-auditor can see a dual-label {financials+legal} memory. JSON has no comments, so this '_comment' key is ignored.
